LRN MAN: DEFENDER OF THE NETWORK
Comic Series by Mike Anderson
=================================

CONTENTS
--------
  issues/         HTML comic book files (Issues #1-5 + Index)
  art/            Artwork and cover images
  Windows/        Windows launcher and installer
  Linux/          Linux launcher and installer
  pdfs/           PDF versions of all 5 issues


QUICK START — READ WITHOUT INSTALLING
--------------------------------------
Just open:  issues/index.html  in any web browser.

  Windows : Double-click  Windows\Launch-LRN-Man.bat
  Linux   : Run           Linux/launch-lrn-man.sh
  Any OS  : Open          issues/index.html  directly in your browser


INSTALL ON WINDOWS
------------------
1. Extract this zip to any folder
2. Double-click  Windows\Install-LRN-Man.bat
3. Follow the prompts
4. A Desktop shortcut and Start Menu entry will be created
5. To uninstall, run the Uninstall shortcut in Start Menu > LRN Man


INSTALL ON LINUX
----------------
1. Extract this zip to any folder
2. Open a terminal in that folder
3. Run:  bash Linux/install-lrn-man.sh
4. An application menu entry will be created
5. Run anywhere with:  lrn-man  (if ~/bin is in your PATH)
6. To uninstall:  bash ~/LRN-Man/uninstall.sh


ISSUES IN THIS SERIES
---------------------
  #1  The Last Son of Maytag         — His Origin. His Mission.
  #2  The Ransomware Wars            — Locked. Encrypted. Under Siege.
  #3  The Botnet Invasion            — When the Machines Rise
  #4  The Social Engineer            — Hook, Line, and Sinker
  #5  The Quantum Conspiracy         — When Encryption Falls, Chaos Reigns


SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS
-------------------
Any modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari).
No internet connection required — completely self-contained.


ABOUT
-----
LRN Man: Defender of the Network is an original cybersecurity
educational comic series. Every tool, every threat, and every
defense technique referenced is real technology used by
security professionals worldwide.

Created by Mike Anderson
"Security Is Not a Product — It Is an Architecture."
